name: Briar Forest School Bus Svc
districts_or_customers: likely Houston ISD or local private schools (unverified)
recent_news: No web presence, no news coverage, no website found. Address (13019 Wickersham Ln, 77077) is a residential property in west Houston, suggesting a sole-proprietor/owner-operator bus operation rather than a fleet contractor.
fit_notes: Not a meaningful tuck-in candidate. Appears to be a single-bus owner-operator using a residential address; no fleet, no website, no contracts visible. Below Summit's platform threshold.

name: Kaylynn School Bus Svc
employees_est: directory says ~31 but address is residential — likely overstated
districts_or_customers: unverified; possibly Houston ISD or local private schools
recent_news: No website, no press, no LinkedIn corporate page. Address (14811 Winterfair Dr, 77082) is a residential property. A separately-named 'Kaylynn's Bus Service' Nextdoor page lists Rosenberg, TX but is unrelated/unconfirmed. Likely a single-operator entity.
fit_notes: Not a viable tuck-in target. Sub-scale owner-operator with no demonstrable fleet or district contracts; well below Summit's M&A threshold.

name: Tona School Bus
recent_news: No website, no press coverage, no corporate filings surfaced. 607 Kilroy St in Houston (77013) is a small east-Houston commercial/residential lot. Appears to be a sole-proprietor school transport business, possibly serving a single private school or church school.
fit_notes: Not a meaningful tuck-in candidate. Single-operator scale, no fleet visibility, no contracts; below Summit's acquisition threshold.

name: DLM School Bus
principals: Henry Drake (former part-owner; Bloom Twp HS Dist 206 board president)
recent_news: Subject of local Patch coverage (Chicago Heights) examining whether Bloom 206 board president Henry Drake had ownership ties to DLM; Henry stated he owned a percentage in 1989-90 but the company is his sister Madelyn Drake's. Originally founded by his father. Now operates out of South Chicago Heights.
fit_notes: Small but legitimate independent family-owned Illinois school-bus operator with three named district contracts in Chicago's south suburbs. Founder-generation transition (father → daughter) already occurred; modest fleet (~10-20). Potential bolt-on if Summit wants a south-suburban Chicago footprint, but small revenue (~$1.5-2.5M) and minority/family-owned legacy may complicate process. Worth a low-priority outreach.

name: North American Central Sch Bus
owner_or_parent: H.I.G. Infrastructure (H.I.G. Capital) — acquired Aug 2021 from Goldner Hawn
fleet_size_est: ~3,000 buses nationally (Idaho location is a single regional facility)
districts_or_customers: (Nampa SD contract terminated after 64 years per 2024 Idaho Ed News)
recent_news: NACSB (HQ Joliet, IL) is the 5th-largest US school-bus operator, ~3,000 buses across IL/IN/WI/MO/KS/PA/ID/IA/TX + Ontario. Acquired by H.I.G. Infrastructure in Aug 2021 from Goldner Hawn. Nampa, ID location is the former Brown Bus Company (founded 1959, acquired by NACSB / Illinois Central School Bus). Lost flagship Nampa SD contract after a 64-year run (2024); contested decision per Idaho Ed News.
fit_notes: NOT a tuck-in target. NACSB is a top-5 national platform owned by H.I.G. Infrastructure (PE) — a direct strategic competitor to Summit. The Nampa facility is a single branch of that platform, not an independent operator. Exclude from outreach. (However, the loss of the Nampa SD contract could create acquisition opportunities for whichever local operator replaced them — worth a separate look at Nampa's new contractor.)
